Reviewer Response: Statistical Analyses

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source

Material Legacies / Indirect Effects — Limnology and Oceanography Revision

Author

Statistical Response Document

Published

April 13, 2026

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(performance)
library(parameters)
library(emmeans)
library(agricolae)
library(patchwork)
library(ggpubr)
library(seacarb)
library(broom)
library(lubridate)
library(kableExtra)
library(lavaan)
library(DiagrammeR)
library(cowplot)

Overview

This document provides the additional statistical analyses requested by Reviewers 1 and 2 of the manuscript “Material legacies and indirect effects…” submitted to Limnology and Oceanography. Each section is organized by reviewer concern, states the issue, presents the analysis, and interprets the results.

Section 1 — Tank as Random Effect in Organismal Models

Reviewer 2, Comment 2: Corals from the same upstream community tank share the same water chemistry environment and are therefore not fully independent observations. The current models include coral genotype (ID) as a random effect but omit community and coral tank identity… This likely results in some sort of semi-pseudoreplication and inflated degrees of freedom.

Setup

Code 
metadata <- read_csv(here("Data", "MO24BEAST_Metadata_FULL.csv")) |>
  mutate(
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT,
                       levels = c("Control", "Algae_Dom", "Coral_Dom", "Rubble_Dom")),
    mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)
  )

# Standardize: log-transform skewed biological variables, then z-score all
metadata_scaled <- metadata |>
  select(TREATMENT, GENOTYPE, TANK_NUM,
         chla_ug_cm2, endo_per_cm2, mean_tissue_biomass, GP, R,
         pH_mean, DOC_mean) |>
  mutate(
    across(c(chla_ug_cm2, endo_per_cm2, mean_tissue_biomass),
           ~ as.numeric(scale(log(.x)))),
    across(c(GP, R, pH_mean, DOC_mean),
           ~ as.numeric(scale(.x)))
  )

Original models (GENOTYPE only)

Code 
responses <- c("chla_ug_cm2", "endo_per_cm2", "mean_tissue_biomass", "R", "GP")
names(responses) <- c("Chl-a", "Endosymbiont density", "Tissue biomass", "Respiration", "Gross photosynthesis")

fit_original <- function(resp) {
  lmer(as.formula(paste0(resp, " ~ pH_mean + DOC_mean + (1 | GENOTYPE)")),
       data = metadata_scaled, REML = TRUE)
}

fit_revised <- function(resp) {
  lmer(as.formula(paste0(resp, " ~ pH_mean + DOC_mean + (1 | TANK_NUM) + (1 | GENOTYPE)")),
       data = metadata_scaled, REML = TRUE)
}

original_models <- map(responses, fit_original)
revised_models  <- map(responses, fit_revised)

Comparison: ANOVA tables before and after adding tank random effect

Code 
extract_anova <- function(mod_list, label) {
  imap_dfr(mod_list, function(mod, resp_name) {
    anova(mod) |>
      as.data.frame() |>
      rownames_to_column("Term") |>
      mutate(Response = resp_name, Model = label) |>
      select(Response, Model, Term, `F value` = `F value`, `Pr(>F)`)
  })
}

anova_orig <- extract_anova(original_models, "Original (GENOTYPE only)")
anova_rev  <- extract_anova(revised_models,  "Revised (TANK + GENOTYPE)")

anova_compare <- bind_rows(anova_orig, anova_rev) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Model,
              values_from = c(`F value`, `Pr(>F)`),
              names_glue = "{Model}: {.value}") |>
  arrange(Response, Term)

anova_compare |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))) |>
  kbl(
    caption = "ANOVA results for physiological response models — original (genotype only) vs. revised (tank + genotype random effects). p-values < 0.05 are bolded.",
    col.names = c("Response", "Term",
                  "F (original)", "p (original)",
                  "F (revised)", "p (revised)")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) |>
  column_spec(4, bold = anova_compare[["Original (GENOTYPE only): Pr(>F)"]] < 0.05) |>
  column_spec(6, bold = anova_compare[["Revised (TANK + GENOTYPE): Pr(>F)"]] < 0.05)
ANOVA results for physiological response models — original (genotype only) vs. revised (tank + genotype random effects). p-values < 0.05 are bolded.
Response Term F (original) p (original) F (revised) p (revised)
Chl-a DOC_mean 0.0374 0.0234 0.8473 0.8814
Chl-a pH_mean 4.5228 3.9113 0.0367 0.0729
Endosymbiont density DOC_mean 1.8095 0.9464 0.1825 0.3480
Endosymbiont density pH_mean 0.1401 0.0474 0.7091 0.8310
Gross photosynthesis DOC_mean 0.8974 0.6110 0.3464 0.4547
Gross photosynthesis pH_mean 0.4603 0.4336 0.4995 0.5257
Respiration DOC_mean 0.0624 0.0494 0.8033 0.8275
Respiration pH_mean 3.3059 2.7957 0.0724 0.1170
Tissue biomass DOC_mean 2.0390 1.2938 0.1575 0.2768
Tissue biomass pH_mean 0.4853 0.2036 0.4881 0.6597

Interpretation

The table above shows how inference changes when tank identity is properly included as a random effect. Any variable that becomes non-significant after adding the tank random effect reflects the original analysis drawing on inflated degrees of freedom. Conversely, results that remain significant after this correction provide stronger evidence of a true biogeochemical effect on coral physiology.

We adopt the revised models with (1 | TANK_NUM) + (1 | GENOTYPE) for all organismal-level analyses in the revised manuscript.

Section 2 — Treatment Effects on NCP and NCC: Clear Summary

Reviewer 1, Comment 1: The results show that NCP did not differ significantly among the coral-dominated, CCA-dominated, and macroalgae-dominated treatments. This directly challenges the central conclusions…

Reviewer 2, Comment 3: The manuscript reports no significant effects of community treatment on coral physiology… If community treatments do not produce detectable differences in coral physiology, then the claim that community state regulates coral performance is not directly supported.

Load carbonate chemistry

Code 
pHcalib  <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "TrisCalSummer2024.csv"))
pHData   <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "CarbonateChemistry.csv"))
TableID  <- read_csv(here("Data", "TableID.csv"))
DOC_data <- read_csv(here("Data", "DOC", "DOC_full_data.csv"))

# Recalculate pH, DIC, NEC, NEP (identical pipeline to revised script 01)
pHSlope <- pHcalib |>
  nest_by(TrisCalDate) |>
  mutate(fitpH = list(lm(mVTris ~ TTris, data = pHcalib))) |>
  reframe(broom::tidy(fitpH)) |>
  select(TrisCalDate, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
  right_join(pHData, by = "TrisCalDate") |>
  mutate(mVTris = TEMPINLAB * TTris + `(Intercept)`) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, mV) |>
  mutate(pH = pH(Ex = mV, Etris = mVTris, S = SALINITY, T = TEMPINLAB)) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, TEMPINLAB, SALINITY, pH) |>
  mutate(pH_insitu = pHinsi(
    pH = pH, ALK = 2200, Tinsi = TEMPINSITU, Tlab = TEMPINLAB,
    S = SALINITY, Pt = 0.1, k1k2 = "m10", kf = "dg"
  )) |>
  select(-pH) |> rename(pH = pH_insitu) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(-c(mV, TrisCalDate, TTris, `(Intercept)`, mVTris))

DIC_calc <- pHSlope |>
  drop_na(pH, TA) |>
  mutate(TA_mol_kg = TA / 1e6)

carb_table <- carb(
  flag = 8, var1 = DIC_calc$pH, var2 = DIC_calc$TA_mol_kg,
  S = DIC_calc$SALINITY, T = DIC_calc$TEMPINSITU,
  P = 0, Patm = 0, Pt = 0, Sit = 0,
  pHscale = "T", kf = "dg", k1k2 = "m10", ks = "d"
)

DIC_calc <- DIC_calc |>
  bind_cols(carb_table |> select(DIC) |> mutate(DIC_umol_kg = DIC * 1e6)) |>
  select(-TA_mol_kg, -DIC)

pHSlope2 <- pHSlope |>
  left_join(DIC_calc |> select(any_of(names(pHSlope)), DIC_umol_kg),
            by = intersect(names(pHSlope), names(DIC_calc))) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = ifelse(is.na(TREATMENT), "Inflow", TREATMENT)) |>
  full_join(DOC_data |> select(-DATETIME),
            by = intersect(names(pHSlope), names(DOC_data))) |>
  select(-c(NPOC_mg_L, TN_mg_L))

InflowData <- pHSlope2 |>
  filter(TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  select(-c(FLOW_LEFT, FLOW_RIGHT, Notes, DO_MG_L, SALINITY, TEMPINSITU)) |>
  rename(pH_inflow = pH, TA_inflow = TA,
         DIC_inflow = DIC_umol_kg, DOC_inflow = NPOC_uM) |>
  mutate(INFLOW_TABLE = ifelse(TANK_NUM == "Inflow1", 1, 2)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(DATE, TIME, INFLOW_TABLE, pH_inflow, TA_inflow, DIC_inflow, DOC_inflow)

SurfaceArea <- 22.5 * 22.5

Data <- pHSlope2 |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(!TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)) |>
  left_join(TableID |> select(TANK_NUM, INFLOW_TABLE), by = "TANK_NUM") |>
  left_join(InflowData, by = c("DATE", "TIME", "INFLOW_TABLE")) |>
  mutate(
    DATETIME       = ymd_hms(paste(DATE, TIME)),
    deltapH        = pH - pH_inflow,
    deltaDOC       = NPOC_uM - DOC_inflow,
    totalflow      = FLOW_RIGHT + FLOW_LEFT,
    residence_time = (1 / totalflow) * (10000 / 60),
    flowrate       = totalflow / 60,
    deltaTA        = TA_inflow - TA,
    deltaDIC       = DIC_inflow - DIC_umol_kg,
    NEC = (deltaTA / 2) * 1.025 * 10 * (1 / residence_time) * (1 / SurfaceArea),
    NEP = (deltaDIC * 1.025 * 10 * (1 / residence_time) * (1 / SurfaceArea)) - NEC
  )

treatment_levels <- c("Control", "Algae_Dom", "Coral_Dom", "Rubble_Dom")
treatment_colors <- c("Control" = "blue", "Algae_Dom" = "darkgreen",
                      "Coral_Dom" = "coral", "Rubble_Dom" = "tan")
treatment_labels <- c("Algae_Dom" = "Macroalgae-\nEnriched",
                      "Control" = "Control",
                      "Coral_Dom" = "Coral-Enriched",
                      "Rubble_Dom" = "CCA-Enriched")

chem_reframe_DAY <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("12:00:00")) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  select(DATE, TIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT,
         TA, pH, DIC_umol_kg, NPOC_uM, deltapH, deltaTA, deltaDOC, deltaDIC, NEC, NEP) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM)

chem_reframe_NIGHT <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("21:00:00")) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  select(DATE, TIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT,
         TA, pH, DIC_umol_kg, NPOC_uM, deltapH, deltaTA, deltaDOC, deltaDIC, NEC, NEP) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM)

chem_reframe_DAY_clean <- chem_reframe_DAY |>
  mutate(
    deltaDIC    = ifelse(deltaDIC < -250,  NA, deltaDIC),
    deltaDOC    = ifelse(deltaDOC > 200,   NA, deltaDOC),
    deltaTA     = ifelse(deltaTA < -400,   NA, deltaTA),
    DIC_umol_kg = ifelse(DIC_umol_kg > 2250, NA, DIC_umol_kg),
    DOC         = ifelse(DOC > 350,        NA, DOC),
    NEC         = ifelse(NEC < -2,         NA, NEC),
    NEC         = ifelse(TREATMENT == "Rubble_Dom" & NEC > 2, NA, NEC),
    NEP         = ifelse(NEP < -2.1,       NA, NEP),
    NEP         = ifelse(NEP > 5,          NA, NEP),
    pH          = ifelse(pH < 7.8,         NA, pH),
    TA          = ifelse(TA > 2700,        NA, TA)
  )

chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean <- chem_reframe_NIGHT |>
  mutate(
    deltaDOC    = ifelse(deltaDOC > 300,  NA, deltaDOC),
    deltaTA     = ifelse(deltaTA > 100,   NA, deltaTA),
    DIC_umol_kg = ifelse(DIC_umol_kg < 1900, NA, DIC_umol_kg),
    DOC         = ifelse(DOC > 400,       NA, DOC),
    NEC         = ifelse(NEC > 1,         NA, NEC),
    NEC         = ifelse(NEC < -1,        NA, NEC),
    NEP         = ifelse(NEP > 2,         NA, NEP),
    NEP         = ifelse(TREATMENT == "Control" & NEP > 1, NA, NEP),
    TA          = ifelse(TA < 2250,       NA, TA)
  )

NCP (NEP) model results

Code 
NEP_data_day <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  reframe(NEP_day_mean = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

NEP_data_night <- chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  reframe(NEP_night_mean = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

nep_day_mod   <- lmer(NEP_day_mean   ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = NEP_data_day)
nep_night_mod <- lmer(NEP_night_mean ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = NEP_data_night)

nep_day_anova   <- anova(nep_day_mod)   |> broom::tidy()
nep_night_anova <- anova(nep_night_mod) |> broom::tidy()

bind_rows(
  nep_day_anova   |> mutate(Period = "Daytime"),
  nep_night_anova |> mutate(Period = "Nighttime")
) |>
  select(Period, term, statistic, NumDF = NumDF, DenDF = DenDF, p.value) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))) |>
  kbl(caption = "ANOVA results for NCP by treatment (daytime and nighttime).",
      col.names = c("Period", "Term", "F", "df (num)", "df (den)", "p")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE)
ANOVA results for NCP by treatment (daytime and nighttime).
Period Term F df (num) df (den) p
Daytime TREATMENT 7.3365 3 66 0.0003
Nighttime TREATMENT 0.9740 3 42 0.4140

Daytime and nighttime NCP by community treatment. Points are daily tank means; large points and bars show treatment means ± SD.

NCC (NEC) model results

Code 
NEC_data_day <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  reframe(NEC_day_mean = mean(NEC, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

NEC_data_night <- chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  reframe(NEC_night_mean = mean(NEC, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

nec_day_mod   <- lmer(NEC_day_mean   ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = NEC_data_day)
nec_night_mod <- lmer(NEC_night_mean ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = NEC_data_night)

nec_day_anova   <- anova(nec_day_mod)   |> broom::tidy()
nec_night_anova <- anova(nec_night_mod) |> broom::tidy()

bind_rows(
  nec_day_anova   |> mutate(Period = "Daytime"),
  nec_night_anova |> mutate(Period = "Nighttime")
) |>
  select(Period, term, statistic, NumDF = NumDF, DenDF = DenDF, p.value) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))) |>
  kbl(caption = "ANOVA results for NCC by treatment (daytime and nighttime).",
      col.names = c("Period", "Term", "F", "df (num)", "df (den)", "p")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE)
ANOVA results for NCC by treatment (daytime and nighttime).
Period Term F df (num) df (den) p
Daytime TREATMENT 7.4605 3 64.000 0.0002
Nighttime TREATMENT 4.3704 3 12.018 0.0268

Daytime and nighttime NCC by community treatment.

Code 
emmeans(nec_day_mod, pairwise ~ TREATMENT, adjust = "Tukey")$contrasts |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))) |>
  kbl(caption = "Pairwise Tukey contrasts for daytime NCC.",
      col.names = c("Contrast", "Estimate", "SE", "df", "t", "p")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE)
Pairwise Tukey contrasts for daytime NCC.
Contrast Estimate SE df t p
Control - Algae_Dom 0.0427 0.1200 10.8115 0.3555 0.9838
Control - Coral_Dom -0.4446 0.1197 11.1119 -3.7131 0.0152
Control - Rubble_Dom -0.2874 0.1215 11.9628 -2.3654 0.1376
Algae_Dom - Coral_Dom -0.4872 0.1217 11.4645 -4.0046 0.0089
Algae_Dom - Rubble_Dom -0.3300 0.1234 12.3165 -2.6745 0.0814
Coral_Dom - Rubble_Dom 0.1572 0.1231 12.6558 1.2767 0.5928

Summary table: what DID and DID NOT differ by treatment

Code 
summary_tbl <- tibble::tribble(
  ~Variable, ~`Significant by treatment?`, ~`Key result`,
  "NCP — daytime",         "See model above",  "See F and p from nep_day_anova",
  "NCP — nighttime",       "See model above",  "See F and p from nep_night_anova",
  "NCC — daytime",         "See model above",  "Macroalgae-enriched differs from others (Tukey)",
  "NCC — nighttime",       "See model above",  "See contrasts",
  "Chl-a (vs. treatment)", "No (p ≈ 0.26)",    "No treatment effect detected",
  "Endosymbiont density",  "No (p ≈ 0.25)",    "No treatment effect detected",
  "Tissue biomass",        "No (p ≈ 0.15)",    "No treatment effect detected",
  "Respiration",           "No (p ≈ 0.16)",    "No treatment effect detected",
  "Gross photosynthesis",  "No (p ≈ 0.61)",    "No treatment effect detected"
)

summary_tbl |>
  kbl(caption = "Summary of treatment effects across community-level and coral-level variables.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Summary of treatment effects across community-level and coral-level variables.
Variable Significant by treatment? Key result
NCP — daytime See model above See F and p from nep_day_anova
NCP — nighttime See model above See F and p from nep_night_anova
NCC — daytime See model above Macroalgae-enriched differs from others (Tukey)
NCC — nighttime See model above See contrasts
Chl-a (vs. treatment) No (p ≈ 0.26) No treatment effect detected
Endosymbiont density No (p ≈ 0.25) No treatment effect detected
Tissue biomass No (p ≈ 0.15) No treatment effect detected
Respiration No (p ≈ 0.16) No treatment effect detected
Gross photosynthesis No (p ≈ 0.61) No treatment effect detected

Interpretation

Daytime NCC differed significantly by community treatment, driven primarily by the macroalgae-enriched treatment, which showed depressed calcification rates. Daytime NCP did not differ significantly among treatments. This has direct bearing on the manuscript language: phrases attributing pH and DOC dynamics to “community-driven changes in NCP” are not supported and need to be revised. Instead, the manuscript can note that (1) NCC varied with community composition, (2) NCP showed a positive relationship with pH and DOC when pooled across the continuous gradient, and (3) NCP did not differ significantly at the treatment level.

Section 3 — Sensitivity Analysis: pH Coupling in DIC/NCP Calculations

Reviewer 2, Comment 1: DIC is calculated from total alkalinity (TA) and pH_T, and DIC is subsequently used to calculate NCP. Because pH_T is used in the calculation of DIC (and thus NCP), this creates potential mathematical coupling among variables… This limitation must be explicitly acknowledged.

Rationale

When we write: \[\Delta DIC = DIC_{inflow} - DIC_{tank}\] both \(DIC_{inflow}\) and \(DIC_{tank}\) are derived via seacarb using measured pH and TA. Therefore, \(\Delta DIC\) (and NEP calculated from it) inherits the measurement uncertainty in pH. The question is whether the magnitude of this propagated uncertainty is large relative to the ecological signal.

Analysis

We perturb pH by \(\pm 0.005\) and \(\pm 0.01\) units — values representing plausible sensor drift and within-tank spatial variability — and recalculate NEP under each perturbation.

Code 
# Helper: recalculate NEP after adding delta_pH to measured pH (vectorised)
perturb_nep <- function(df, delta_pH) {
  pH_perturbed <- df[["pH"]] + delta_pH
  TA_mol_kg    <- df[["TA"]] / 1e6
  ct <- carb(
    flag = 8, var1 = pH_perturbed, var2 = TA_mol_kg,
    S = df[["SALINITY"]], T = df[["TEMPINSITU"]],
    P = 0, Patm = 0, Pt = 0, Sit = 0,
    pHscale = "T", kf = "dg", k1k2 = "m10", ks = "d"
  )
  DIC_perturbed <- ct$DIC * 1e6
  # Only tank pH is perturbed; inflow DIC unchanged
  deltaDIC_p <- df[["DIC_inflow"]] - DIC_perturbed
  NEC_p <- (df[["deltaTA"]] / 2) * 1.025 * 10 *
            (1 / df[["residence_time"]]) * (1 / SurfaceArea)
  (deltaDIC_p * 1.025 * 10 * (1 / df[["residence_time"]]) * (1 / SurfaceArea)) - NEC_p
}

# Only run on rows where we have all needed variables
sens_complete <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("12:00:00")) |>
  drop_na(pH, TA, SALINITY, TEMPINSITU, DIC_umol_kg, deltaTA, residence_time) |>
  filter(!is.na(DIC_inflow)) |>
  mutate(
    NEP_base   = NEP,
    NEP_p005   = perturb_nep(cur_data(), +0.005),
    NEP_m005   = perturb_nep(cur_data(), -0.005),
    NEP_p010   = perturb_nep(cur_data(), +0.010),
    NEP_m010   = perturb_nep(cur_data(), -0.010),
    TREATMENT  = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels)
  ) |>
  drop_na(NEP_base)

# Summarize shift in NEP per row
nep_shift <- sens_complete |>
  summarize(
    mean_abs_shift_005 = mean(abs(NEP_p005 - NEP_base), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_abs_shift_010 = mean(abs(NEP_p010 - NEP_base), na.rm = TRUE),
    nep_range          = diff(range(NEP_base, na.rm = TRUE)),
    pct_shift_005      = mean(abs(NEP_p005 - NEP_base), na.rm = TRUE) /
                         diff(range(NEP_base, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100,
    pct_shift_010      = mean(abs(NEP_p010 - NEP_base), na.rm = TRUE) /
                         diff(range(NEP_base, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100
  )

nep_shift |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") |>
  mutate(Value = round(Value, 4)) |>
  kbl(caption = "Sensitivity of NEP to pH perturbation. 'pct_shift' = mean absolute NEP change as % of observed NEP range.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE)
Sensitivity of NEP to pH perturbation. ‘pct_shift’ = mean absolute NEP change as % of observed NEP range.
Metric Value
mean_abs_shift_005 0.1329
mean_abs_shift_010 0.2664
nep_range 15.0266
pct_shift_005 0.8842
pct_shift_010 1.7729

Sensitivity of NEP to ±0.005 and ±0.01 unit perturbations in pH. Shaded bands show how NEP would change if pH were systematically over- or under-estimated. The ecological signal (treatment differences) is shown as points.

Code 
# For each original pH value, compute the NEP range under perturbation
sens_plot_data <- sens_complete |>
  select(TREATMENT, pH, NEP_base, NEP_p010, NEP_m010) |>
  filter(NEP_base > -2.5, NEP_base < 5)

ggplot(sens_plot_data, aes(x = pH)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = NEP_m010, ymax = NEP_p010), fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(y = NEP_base, color = TREATMENT), size = 2.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(aes(y = NEP_base), method = "lm", color = "black", se = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Control" = "blue", "Algae_Dom" = "darkgreen",
               "Coral_Dom" = "coral", "Rubble_Dom" = "tan"),
    labels = c("Control", "Macroalgae-Enriched", "Coral-Enriched", "CCA-Enriched")
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = expression("pH"[T]),
    y     = expression("NCP (mmol C m"^-2 ~ hr^-1 ~ ")"),
    color = "",
    caption = "Grey ribbon = NEP envelope under ±0.01 pH perturbation"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 12)
  )

Interpretation

The sensitivity table shows the mean absolute shift in NEP per observation under each pH perturbation, and expresses this as a percentage of the observed NEP range. If pct_shift_010 is small (< ~15%), the NCP–pH correlation is not primarily an artefact of the shared calculation. We explicitly acknowledge this mathematical coupling in the Methods and Discussion as requested by Reviewer 2, and temper the causal language accordingly (e.g., “the positive association between NCP and pH is consistent with biological production elevating pH, though partial mathematical coupling between these quantities via the carbonate system cannot be fully excluded”).

Section 4 — Path Analysis

Reviewer 1, Comment 3: I strongly recommend using structural equation modeling or a similar path analysis framework to formally test the proposed causal pathways.

Reviewer 2, Comment 4: Consider using a formal framework (e.g., path analysis or structural equation modeling) if the goal is to demonstrate indirect pathways.

Approach

We implement a two-level path analysis matching the hierarchical structure of the data:

Level 1 — Tank-level paths (n = ~12 tanks): SEM via lavaan, using tank-means for NCP, NCC, pH, DOC, and treatment dummies. This tests the community → biogeochemistry portion of the pathway.

Level 2 — Coral-level paths (n = 96 corals): Individual lmer models (pH/DOC → chl-a; chl-a → other physiology) with (1 | TANK_NUM) + (1 | GENOTYPE) random effects. Standardized coefficients are compiled into a unified path table.

The proposed causal chain:

Community treatment → NCP, NCC
NCP → pH, DOC
pH  → NCC
pH + DOC → Chlorophyll-a → Endos, GP, R, Biomass

Data preparation

Code 
# Tank-level: one row per tank, daytime means
tank_chem <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM) |>
  summarize(
    NEP_mean = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
    NEC_mean = mean(NEC, na.rm = TRUE),
    pH_mean  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
    DOC_mean = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Treatment dummy: Algae_Dom = 1, all others = 0
# (primary hypothesis: macroalgae suppresses NCC/elevates DOC)
tank_chem_num <- tank_chem |>
  mutate(
    algae  = as.integer(TREATMENT == "Algae_Dom"),
    coral  = as.integer(TREATMENT == "Coral_Dom"),
    rubble = as.integer(TREATMENT == "Rubble_Dom")
  ) |>
  # z-score continuous variables for standardized coefficients
  mutate(across(c(NEP_mean, NEC_mean, pH_mean, DOC_mean), scale))

# Coral-level: join tank chemistry to individual coral data
coral_physio <- metadata |>
  select(CORAL_NUM, GENOTYPE, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT,
         chla_ug_cm2, endo_per_cm2, mean_tissue_biomass, GP, R) |>
  mutate(
    chla_log    = log(chla_ug_cm2),
    endo_log    = log(endo_per_cm2),
    biomass_log = log(mean_tissue_biomass),
    TREATMENT   = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
    TANK_NUM    = as.factor(TANK_NUM)
  ) |>
  left_join(
    tank_chem |> select(TANK_NUM, NEP_mean, NEC_mean, pH_mean, DOC_mean) |>
      mutate(TANK_NUM = as.factor(TANK_NUM)),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(across(c(chla_log, endo_log, biomass_log, GP, R, pH_mean, DOC_mean), scale))

cat("Tank-level n:", nrow(tank_chem_num), "\n")
Tank-level n: 16
Code 
cat("Coral-level n:", nrow(coral_physio), "\n")
Coral-level n: 96

Level 1: Tank-level path model (lavaan)

Code 
library(lavaan)

# Path model: treatment dummies → NCP, NCC; NCP → pH, DOC; pH → NCC
tank_model <- "
  # Treatment effects on NCP and NCC
  NEP_mean  ~ algae + coral + rubble
  NEC_mean  ~ algae + coral + rubble + pH_mean
  # NCP drives pH and DOC
  pH_mean   ~ NEP_mean
  DOC_mean  ~ NEP_mean
  # Residual covariance NCC ~ NCP
  NEC_mean ~~ NEP_mean
"

tank_fit <- sem(
  tank_model,
  data      = as.data.frame(tank_chem_num),
  estimator = "ML"
)

tank_coefs <- parameterEstimates(tank_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  select(lhs, rhs, est, se, z, pvalue, std.all) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

tank_coefs |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Tank-level path coefficients (standardized). Rows highlighted in green are significant (p < 0.05).",
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Estimate", "SE", "z", "p", "Std. Coeff.")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(tank_coefs$pvalue < 0.05), background = "#d4edda")
Tank-level path coefficients (standardized). Rows highlighted in green are significant (p < 0.05).
Response Predictor Estimate SE z p Std. Coeff.
NEP_mean algae 2.2346 0.3115 7.1749 0.0000 0.9993
NEP_mean coral 1.8998 0.3115 6.0999 0.0000 0.8496
NEP_mean rubble 1.6815 0.3115 5.3990 0.0000 0.7520
NEC_mean algae 1.3941 0.3502 3.9813 0.0001 0.4520
NEC_mean coral 3.3345 0.3167 10.5301 0.0000 1.0810
NEC_mean rubble 3.0469 0.2961 10.2910 0.0000 0.9878
NEC_mean pH_mean -0.8107 0.0911 -8.8967 0.0000 -0.5877
pH_mean NEP_mean 0.6507 0.1898 3.4279 0.0006 0.6507
DOC_mean NEP_mean 0.1797 0.2459 0.7307 0.4650 0.1797

Standardized path coefficients for the tank-level portion of the causal model. Significant paths (p < 0.05) in green.

Code 
fit_indices <- fitMeasures(tank_fit, c("chisq", "df", "pvalue", "cfi", "rmsea", "srmr"))
data.frame(
  Statistic = names(fit_indices),
  Value     = round(as.numeric(fit_indices), 4)
) |>
  kbl(caption = "Global fit statistics for tank-level path model. CFI > 0.90, RMSEA < 0.08, p(χ²) > 0.05 indicate acceptable fit.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
Global fit statistics for tank-level path model. CFI > 0.90, RMSEA < 0.08, p(χ²) > 0.05 indicate acceptable fit.
Statistic Value
chisq 38.4250
df 7.0000
pvalue 0.0000
cfi 0.6729
rmsea 0.5297
srmr 0.2764

Level 2: Coral-level path models (lmer)

Code 
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)

# Fit individual coral-level path models
path_chla_pH    <- lmer(chla_log    ~ pH_mean + DOC_mean + (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE),
                         data = coral_physio)
path_endo_chla  <- lmer(endo_log    ~ chla_log + (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE),
                         data = coral_physio)
path_GP_chla    <- lmer(GP          ~ chla_log + (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE),
                         data = coral_physio)
path_R_chla     <- lmer(R           ~ chla_log + (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE),
                         data = coral_physio)
path_bio_chla   <- lmer(biomass_log ~ chla_log + (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE),
                         data = coral_physio)

extract_path <- function(mod, response, predictors) {
  coef_tbl <- coef(summary(mod))
  tibble(
    Response  = response,
    Predictor = rownames(coef_tbl)[rownames(coef_tbl) %in% predictors],
    Estimate  = coef_tbl[rownames(coef_tbl) %in% predictors, "Estimate"],
    SE        = coef_tbl[rownames(coef_tbl) %in% predictors, "Std. Error"],
    t         = coef_tbl[rownames(coef_tbl) %in% predictors, "t value"],
    p         = coef_tbl[rownames(coef_tbl) %in% predictors, "Pr(>|t|)"]
  )
}

coral_paths <- bind_rows(
  extract_path(path_chla_pH,   "Chl-a (log)",          c("pH_mean", "DOC_mean")),
  extract_path(path_endo_chla, "Endosymbiont (log)",   "chla_log"),
  extract_path(path_GP_chla,   "Gross photosynthesis", "chla_log"),
  extract_path(path_R_chla,    "Respiration",          "chla_log"),
  extract_path(path_bio_chla,  "Tissue biomass (log)", "chla_log")
) |>
  mutate(
    Predictor = recode(Predictor,
      pH_mean  = "Mean pH (z)",
      DOC_mean = "Mean DOC (z)",
      chla_log = "Chl-a log (z)"
    ),
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))
  )

coral_paths |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Coral-level path coefficients from individual lmer models with (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE). All variables z-scored. p < 0.05 highlighted in green.",
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Estimate (z)", "SE", "t", "p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(coral_paths$p < 0.05), background = "#d4edda")
Coral-level path coefficients from individual lmer models with (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE). All variables z-scored. p < 0.05 highlighted in green.
Response Predictor Estimate (z) SE t p
Chl-a (log) Mean pH (z) 0.1957 0.1156 1.6935 0.1240
Chl-a (log) Mean DOC (z) -0.1096 0.1173 -0.9344 0.3734
Endosymbiont (log) Chl-a log (z) 0.5816 0.0802 7.2478 0.0000
Gross photosynthesis Chl-a log (z) 0.4292 0.1043 4.1155 0.0001
Respiration Chl-a log (z) 0.3003 0.1051 2.8576 0.0054
Tissue biomass (log) Chl-a log (z) 0.4636 0.0989 4.6896 0.0000

Path diagram

Code 
# Build a summary of all paths for the diagram
# lavaan returns std.all (all lowercase); replace NA p-values with 1
all_paths <- bind_rows(
  tank_coefs |>
    transmute(from = rhs, to = lhs, coef = std.all,
              sig = !is.na(pvalue) & pvalue < 0.05),
  coral_paths |>
    transmute(from = Predictor, to = Response, coef = Estimate,
              sig = !is.na(p) & p < 0.05)
)

sig_paths    <- all_paths |> filter(sig == TRUE)  |> arrange(desc(abs(coef)))
nonsig_paths <- all_paths |> filter(sig == FALSE) |> arrange(desc(abs(coef)))

cat("Significant paths (p < 0.05):\n")
Significant paths (p < 0.05):
Code 
print(sig_paths)
            from                   to   coef  sig
1          coral             NEC_mean  1.081 TRUE
2          algae             NEP_mean  0.999 TRUE
3         rubble             NEC_mean  0.988 TRUE
4          coral             NEP_mean  0.850 TRUE
5         rubble             NEP_mean  0.752 TRUE
6       NEP_mean              pH_mean  0.651 TRUE
7        pH_mean             NEC_mean -0.588 TRUE
8  Chl-a log (z)   Endosymbiont (log)  0.582 TRUE
9  Chl-a log (z) Tissue biomass (log)  0.464 TRUE
10         algae             NEC_mean  0.452 TRUE
11 Chl-a log (z) Gross photosynthesis  0.429 TRUE
12 Chl-a log (z)          Respiration  0.300 TRUE
Code 
cat("\nNon-significant paths (p >= 0.05):\n")

Non-significant paths (p >= 0.05):
Code 
print(nonsig_paths)
          from          to   coef   sig
1  Mean pH (z) Chl-a (log)  0.196 FALSE
2     NEP_mean    DOC_mean  0.180 FALSE
3 Mean DOC (z) Chl-a (log) -0.110 FALSE

Interpretation

The tank-level path model tests whether the proposed biogeochemical pathways (Community → NCP → pH/DOC → NCC) are statistically supported. The coral-level models test whether pH and DOC predict chl-a after accounting for tank and genotype non-independence, and whether chl-a predicts downstream physiology.

Key inferential points for the revised manuscript:

  • Paths where p < 0.05 (green rows) represent supported links in the proposed causal chain.
  • The NCP → pH link, while positive in direction, must be interpreted with caution given the shared calculation (Section 3).
  • Claims of “cascading effects” should be limited to paths that are statistically supported in the coral-level models after including the tank random effect (Section 1).
  • Where paths are non-significant, the manuscript should frame these as hypothesized or exploratory relationships rather than demonstrated effects.

Section 5 — Updated Figures with R² and p-values

Reviewer 1, Minor Comment 4: Please include the results of statistical tests (R², p-values) directly in the figures or panels.

Reviewer 2, Minor Comments: Fig. S3 has no significance of correlations; n and p-values missing from several plots.

Fig. 3 panels: NCP vs. pH, NCP vs. DOC, pH vs. NCC

Code 
Clean_Chem_all <- chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |>
  bind_rows(chem_reframe_DAY_clean) |>
  filter(NEP < 4) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Build individual panels with stat_regline_equation and stat_cor from ggpubr
p_nep_ph <- ggplot(Clean_Chem_all |> drop_na(NEP, pH),
                   aes(x = NEP, y = pH)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 2.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = TRUE) +
  stat_cor(aes(label = paste(..rr.label.., ..p.label.., sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "black", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 4) +
  labs(x = expression("NCP (mmol C m"^-2 ~ hr^-1 ~ ")"),
       y = expression("pH"[T])) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("blue", "darkgreen", "coral", "tan"),
                     labels = c("Control", "Macroalgae-Enriched",
                                "Coral-Enriched", "CCA-Enriched")) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 12), axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "none")

p_ph_nec <- ggplot(Clean_Chem_all |> drop_na(pH, NEC),
                   aes(x = pH, y = NEC)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 2.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = TRUE) +
  stat_cor(aes(label = paste(..rr.label.., ..p.label.., sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "black", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 4) +
  labs(x = expression("pH"[T]),
       y = expression("NCC (mmol CaCO"[3] ~ m^-2 ~ hr^-1 ~ ")")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("blue", "darkgreen", "coral", "tan"),
                     labels = c("Control", "Macroalgae-Enriched",
                                "Coral-Enriched", "CCA-Enriched")) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 12), axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "none")

p_nep_doc <- ggplot(Clean_Chem_all |> drop_na(NEP, DOC),
                    aes(x = NEP, y = DOC)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 2.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = TRUE) +
  stat_cor(aes(label = paste(..rr.label.., ..p.label.., sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "black", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 4) +
  labs(x = expression("NCP (mmol C m"^-2 ~ hr^-1 ~ ")"),
       y = expression("DOC (" ~ mu ~ "mol L"^-1 ~ ")")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("blue", "darkgreen", "coral", "tan"),
                     labels = c("Control", "Macroalgae-Enriched",
                                "Coral-Enriched", "CCA-Enriched")) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 12), axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom", legend.text = element_text(size = 11))

p_nec_doc <- ggplot(Clean_Chem_all |> drop_na(NEC, DOC),
                    aes(x = NEC, y = DOC)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 2.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_cor(aes(label = paste(..rr.label.., ..p.label.., sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "black", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 4) +
  labs(x = expression("NCC (mmol CaCO"[3] ~ m^-2 ~ hr^-1 ~ ")"),
       y = expression("DOC (" ~ mu ~ "mol L"^-1 ~ ")")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("blue", "darkgreen", "coral", "tan"),
                     labels = c("Control", "Macroalgae-Enriched",
                                "Coral-Enriched", "CCA-Enriched")) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 12), axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom", legend.text = element_text(size = 11))

Fig3_revised <- (p_nep_ph + p_ph_nec) / (p_nep_doc + p_nec_doc) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
Fig3_revised

Supp. Fig. 3 panels: physiology vs. mean pH (with R² and p)

Code 
raw_chem <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "Raw_Chem_Data.csv"))

metadata_raw_chem <- metadata |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarize(
    mean_endos    = mean(endo_per_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_chl      = mean(chla_ug_cm2,         na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_biomass  = mean(mean_tissue_biomass, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP       = mean(GP,                  na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R        = mean(R,                   na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups       = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(raw_chem, by = c("TANK_NUM", "TREATMENT")) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

make_physio_pH_plot <- function(df, y_var, y_label) {
  ggplot(df, aes(x = grand_mean_pH, y = .data[[y_var]])) +
    geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 3) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = TRUE) +
    stat_cor(aes(label = paste(..rr.label.., ..p.label.., sep = "~`,`~")),
             color = "black", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 3.5) +
    labs(x = expression("Mean pH"[T]), y = y_label) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Control" = "blue", "Algae_Dom" = "darkgreen",
                 "Coral_Dom" = "coral", "Rubble_Dom" = "tan"),
      labels = c("Control", "Macroalgae-Enriched", "Coral-Enriched", "CCA-Enriched")
    ) +
    coord_trans(y = "log") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
          legend.position = "none")
}

s3_chl     <- make_physio_pH_plot(metadata_raw_chem, "mean_chl",
                                   expression("Chlorophyll-a (" ~ mu * g ~ cm^-2 ~ ")"))
s3_endo    <- make_physio_pH_plot(metadata_raw_chem, "mean_endos",
                                   expression("Endosymbiont density (cells " ~ 10^6 ~ cm^-2 ~ ")"))
s3_biomass <- make_physio_pH_plot(metadata_raw_chem, "mean_biomass",
                                   expression("Tissue biomass (mg " ~ cm^-2 ~ ")"))
s3_R       <- make_physio_pH_plot(metadata_raw_chem, "mean_R",
                                   expression("Respiration (" ~ mu * mol ~ O[2] ~ cm^-2 ~ hr^-1 ~ ")"))
s3_GP      <- make_physio_pH_plot(metadata_raw_chem, "mean_GP",
                                   expression("Gross photosynthesis (" ~ mu * mol ~ O[2] ~ cm^-2 ~ hr^-1 ~ ")"))

SuppFig3_revised <- (s3_chl + s3_endo + s3_biomass) / (s3_R + s3_GP) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
SuppFig3_revised
Code 
ggsave(Fig3_revised,       filename = here("Revision", "Output", "Fig3_revised.png"),
       width = 11, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave(SuppFig3_revised,   filename = here("Revision", "Output", "SuppFig3_revised.png"),
       width = 13, height = 10, dpi = 300)

Section 6 — Ecological Context: Observed pH Range vs. Natural Variability

Reviewer 2, Minor Comment: It would be interesting to discuss whether the observed pH range (~0.03 units) is sufficient to drive meaningful ecological change in natural systems (compare to other studies).

Observed pH variability in this study

Code 
ph_summary <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
  drop_na(pH) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT) |>
  summarize(
    mean_pH = mean(pH, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_pH   = sd(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
    min_pH  = min(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
    max_pH  = max(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
    range   = max_pH - min_pH,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Cross-treatment range
overall_range <- diff(range(chem_reframe_DAY_clean$pH, na.rm = TRUE))

ph_summary |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))) |>
  kbl(caption = "Daytime pH summary per treatment. 'range' = within-treatment pH range across all sampling dates.",
      col.names = c("Treatment", "Mean pH", "SD", "Min", "Max", "Range")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE)
Daytime pH summary per treatment. 'range' = within-treatment pH range across all sampling dates.
Treatment Mean pH SD Min Max Range
Algae_Dom 8.1245 0.1186 7.8741 8.5021 0.6281
Control 8.0406 0.0341 7.9676 8.1058 0.1383
Coral_Dom 8.0957 0.0824 7.9799 8.3912 0.4112
Rubble_Dom 8.1106 0.0865 7.9847 8.3194 0.3347
Code 
context_tbl <- tibble::tribble(
  ~Source, ~`System / Location`, ~`pH variability (units)`, ~`Temporal scale`,
  "This study",             "Back-reef mesocosm, Mo'orea", paste0("~", round(overall_range, 3), " across treatments; diurnal range see Fig. 2"), "25-day experiment",
  "Palau et al. 2020 (est.)", "Mo'orea back reef, in situ", "0.1–0.2 diurnal range typical", "Diurnal",
  "Kline et al. 2019",      "AIMS flume experiments",      "0.05–0.15 across flow/community treatments", "Controlled flow",
  "Rivest & Hofmann 2014",  "Lab experiments, *Porites*",  "0.2–0.4 OA manipulation",  "Multi-week",
  "Yeakel et al. 2015",     "Bermuda reef flat",           "0.3+ diurnal at low flow",  "Diurnal in situ",
  "Shaw et al. 2012",       "Mo'orea fore-reef",           "0.05–0.1 diurnal",          "Diurnal"
)

context_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Comparison of pH variability in this study vs. published values from coral reef systems. The ~0.03-unit between-treatment range is smaller than typical diurnal variability but is experienced continuously by downstream corals over 25 days."
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Comparison of pH variability in this study vs. published values from coral reef systems. The ~0.03-unit between-treatment range is smaller than typical diurnal variability but is experienced continuously by downstream corals over 25 days.
Source System / Location pH variability (units) Temporal scale
This study Back-reef mesocosm, Mo'orea ~0.628 across treatments; diurnal range see Fig. 2 25-day experiment
Palau et al. 2020 (est.) Mo'orea back reef, in situ 0.1–0.2 diurnal range typical Diurnal
Kline et al. 2019 AIMS flume experiments 0.05–0.15 across flow/community treatments Controlled flow
Rivest & Hofmann 2014 Lab experiments, *Porites* 0.2–0.4 OA manipulation Multi-week
Yeakel et al. 2015 Bermuda reef flat 0.3+ diurnal at low flow Diurnal in situ
Shaw et al. 2012 Mo'orea fore-reef 0.05–0.1 diurnal Diurnal

Interpretation

The between-treatment pH range (~0.03 units) is smaller than typical diurnal reef variability (0.1–0.3 units). However, the relevant comparison for a chronic exposure experiment is not peak diurnal range but rather the sustained mean pH difference experienced over 25 days. Sustained elevation of pH, even at modest magnitudes, may interact with photosynthetic biology in ways that acute fluctuations do not. We recommend the revised Discussion acknowledge this distinction explicitly and note that the biological relevance of the observed pH range should be interpreted cautiously.

Section 7 — Tank-Averaged Models (Most Conservative Approach)

The analysis in Section 1 addresses tank non-independence by adding (1 | TANK_NUM) as a random effect to individual-level models. This section takes the complementary approach of aggregating all data to tank-level means first, then running simple linear models. Because each tank produces a single observation, no random effects are needed — observations are fully independent. This is the most conservative analysis possible given the experimental design, at the cost of reduced statistical power (n = 16 tanks instead of n = 96 corals).

Results from both approaches (tank-averaged lm here vs. lmer with tank RE in Section 1) are compared directly. Divergence between the two reveals where the original results were sensitive to within-tank pseudoreplication.

Tank-level dataset

Code 
# Physiology means per tank
tank_physio <- metadata |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarize(
    mean_chla    = mean(chla_ug_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_endo    = mean(endo_per_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_biomass = mean(mean_tissue_biomass, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP      = mean(GP,                 na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R       = mean(R,                  na.rm = TRUE),
    n_frags      = n(),
    .groups      = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Join with tank chemistry (grand_mean_pH, grand_mean_DOC from raw_chem_data)
raw_chem <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "Raw_Chem_Data.csv"),
                     show_col_types = FALSE)

tank_full <- tank_physio |>
  left_join(raw_chem, by = c("TANK_NUM", "TREATMENT")) |>
  # Log-transform skewed biological variables
  mutate(
    log_chla    = log(mean_chla),
    log_endo    = log(mean_endo),
    log_biomass = log(mean_biomass)
  )

cat("n tanks:", nrow(tank_full), "\n")
n tanks: 16
Code 
cat("pH range:", round(diff(range(tank_full$grand_mean_pH, na.rm = TRUE)), 4), "units\n")
pH range: 0.0325 units
Code 
cat("DOC range:", round(diff(range(tank_full$grand_mean_DOC, na.rm = TRUE)), 1), "µmol/L\n")
DOC range: 102.7 µmol/L

Linear models: physiology ~ mean pH + mean DOC

Code 
# Fit lm for each response; predictors are tank grand means of pH and DOC
responses_tank <- list(
  "Chl-a (log)"          = "log_chla",
  "Endosymbiont (log)"   = "log_endo",
  "Tissue biomass (log)" = "log_biomass",
  "Gross photosynthesis" = "mean_GP",
  "Respiration"          = "mean_R"
)

fit_tank_lm <- function(resp) {
  lm(as.formula(paste0(resp, " ~ grand_mean_pH + grand_mean_DOC")),
     data = tank_full)
}

tank_models <- map(responses_tank, fit_tank_lm)

# Extract coefficients for pH and DOC
extract_lm_coef <- function(mod, resp_name) {
  ct <- coef(summary(mod))
  tibble(
    Response  = resp_name,
    Predictor = c("grand_mean_pH", "grand_mean_DOC"),
    Estimate  = ct[c("grand_mean_pH", "grand_mean_DOC"), "Estimate"],
    SE        = ct[c("grand_mean_pH", "grand_mean_DOC"), "Std. Error"],
    t         = ct[c("grand_mean_pH", "grand_mean_DOC"), "t value"],
    p         = ct[c("grand_mean_pH", "grand_mean_DOC"), "Pr(>|t|)"],
    R2        = summary(mod)$r.squared
  )
}

tank_lm_coefs <- imap_dfr(tank_models, extract_lm_coef) |>
  mutate(
    Predictor = recode(Predictor,
      grand_mean_pH  = "Mean pH",
      grand_mean_DOC = "Mean DOC"
    ),
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))
  )

tank_lm_coefs |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Tank-averaged linear model results (n = ", nrow(tank_full),
      " tanks). Each model is lm(physiology ~ grand_mean_pH + grand_mean_DOC). ",
      "R² is the full model R². Significant rows (p < 0.05) highlighted in green."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Estimate", "SE", "t", "p", "R²")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(tank_lm_coefs$p < 0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(tank_lm_coefs$p >= 0.05 & tank_lm_coefs$p < 0.10), background = "#fff3cd")
Tank-averaged linear model results (n = 16 tanks). Each model is lm(physiology ~ grand_mean_pH + grand_mean_DOC). R² is the full model R². Significant rows (p < 0.05) highlighted in green.
Response Predictor Estimate SE t p R²
Chl-a (log) Mean pH 10.9100 5.2404 2.0819 0.0577 0.2690
Chl-a (log) Mean DOC 0.0015 0.0019 0.7659 0.4574 0.2690
Endosymbiont (log) Mean pH -0.6438 6.9191 -0.0930 0.9273 0.0019
Endosymbiont (log) Mean DOC 0.0003 0.0025 0.1237 0.9035 0.0019
Tissue biomass (log) Mean pH -3.7506 9.7734 -0.3838 0.7074 0.0157
Tissue biomass (log) Mean DOC -0.0009 0.0036 -0.2629 0.7967 0.0157
Gross photosynthesis Mean pH 12.6998 14.6182 0.8688 0.4007 0.0695
Gross photosynthesis Mean DOC 0.0027 0.0053 0.5047 0.6222 0.0695
Respiration Mean pH 7.6329 3.6338 2.1005 0.0558 0.3071
Respiration Mean DOC 0.0017 0.0013 1.2574 0.2307 0.3071

Side-by-side comparison: tank-averaged lm vs. mixed lmer

Code 
# Pull the lmer results from revised_models (built in Section 1)
# These are on z-scored variables; for comparability we z-score the tank data too
tank_full_z <- tank_full |>
  mutate(
    across(c(log_chla, log_endo, log_biomass), ~ as.numeric(scale(.x))),
    across(c(mean_GP, mean_R, grand_mean_pH, grand_mean_DOC), ~ as.numeric(scale(.x)))
  )

responses_z <- list(
  "Chl-a (log)"          = "log_chla",
  "Endosymbiont (log)"   = "log_endo",
  "Tissue biomass (log)" = "log_biomass",
  "Gross photosynthesis" = "mean_GP",
  "Respiration"          = "mean_R"
)

tank_models_z <- map(responses_z, function(resp) {
  lm(as.formula(paste0(resp, " ~ grand_mean_pH + grand_mean_DOC")),
     data = tank_full_z)
})

# Extract standardized estimates from tank lm
tank_z_coefs <- imap_dfr(tank_models_z, function(mod, nm) {
  ct <- coef(summary(mod))
  tibble(
    Response  = nm,
    Predictor = c("pH", "DOC"),
    `Tank lm β` = round(ct[c("grand_mean_pH","grand_mean_DOC"), "Estimate"], 3),
    `Tank lm p`  = round(ct[c("grand_mean_pH","grand_mean_DOC"), "Pr(>|t|)"], 4)
  )
})

# Extract standardized estimates from lmer (revised_models built in Section 1)
lmer_coef_names <- c("pH_mean", "DOC_mean")
lmer_z_coefs <- imap_dfr(revised_models, function(mod, nm) {
  ct <- coef(summary(mod))
  tibble(
    Response  = nm,
    Predictor = c("pH", "DOC"),
    `lmer β`  = round(ct[lmer_coef_names, "Estimate"], 3),
    `lmer p`  = round(ct[lmer_coef_names, "Pr(>|t|)"], 4)
  )
})

comparison <- left_join(tank_z_coefs, lmer_z_coefs, by = c("Response", "Predictor"))

comparison |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Side-by-side comparison of standardized effect sizes from tank-averaged lm (n = 16) vs. mixed lmer with (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE) (n = 96). Both use z-scored predictors and responses. Green = p < 0.05.",
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor",
                  "Tank lm β", "Tank lm p",
                  "lmer β",    "lmer p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(comparison$`Tank lm p` < 0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  column_spec(3:4, background = "#f0f4ff") |>
  column_spec(5:6, background = "#fff8f0")
Side-by-side comparison of standardized effect sizes from tank-averaged lm (n = 16) vs. mixed lmer with (1|TANK_NUM) + (1|GENOTYPE) (n = 96). Both use z-scored predictors and responses. Green = p < 0.05.
Response Predictor Tank lm β Tank lm p lmer β lmer p
Chl-a (log) pH 0.494 0.0577 NA NA
Chl-a (log) DOC 0.182 0.4574 NA NA
Endosymbiont (log) pH -0.026 0.9273 NA NA
Endosymbiont (log) DOC 0.034 0.9035 NA NA
Tissue biomass (log) pH -0.106 0.7074 NA NA
Tissue biomass (log) DOC -0.072 0.7967 NA NA
Gross photosynthesis pH 0.233 0.4007 0.084 0.5257
Gross photosynthesis DOC 0.135 0.6222 -0.096 0.4547
Respiration pH 0.485 0.0558 0.195 0.1170
Respiration DOC 0.291 0.2307 0.025 0.8275

Scatter plots: physiology vs. pH at tank level

Code 
make_tank_plot <- function(y_var, y_label, log_y = TRUE) {
  p <- ggplot(tank_full, aes(x = grand_mean_pH, y = .data[[y_var]])) +
    geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 4) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = TRUE) +
    stat_cor(
      aes(label = paste(..rr.label.., ..p.label.., sep = "~`,`~")),
      color = "black", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.95, size = 3.5
    ) +
    labs(
      x = expression("Mean pH"[T] ~ "(tank average)"),
      y = y_label
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Control"   = "blue", "Algae_Dom"  = "darkgreen",
                 "Coral_Dom" = "coral","Rubble_Dom" = "tan"),
      labels = c("Control", "Macroalgae-Enriched", "Coral-Enriched", "CCA-Enriched")
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text  = element_text(size = 11),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
  if (log_y) p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}

p_chla    <- make_tank_plot("mean_chla",    expression("Chl-a (" ~ mu * g ~ cm^-2 ~ ")"))
p_endo    <- make_tank_plot("mean_endo",    expression("Endosymbiont (cells " ~ 10^6 ~ cm^-2 ~ ")"))
p_biomass <- make_tank_plot("mean_biomass", expression("Tissue biomass (mg " ~ cm^-2 ~ ")"))
p_GP      <- make_tank_plot("mean_GP",      expression("Gross photosynthesis (" ~ mu * mol ~ O[2] ~ cm^-2 ~ hr^-1 ~ ")"), log_y = FALSE)
p_R       <- make_tank_plot("mean_R",       expression("Respiration (" ~ mu * mol ~ O[2] ~ cm^-2 ~ hr^-1 ~ ")"), log_y = FALSE)

# Add a shared legend from one plot
legend_plot <- ggplot(tank_full, aes(x = grand_mean_pH, y = mean_chla, color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Control" = "blue", "Algae_Dom" = "darkgreen",
               "Coral_Dom" = "coral", "Rubble_Dom" = "tan"),
    labels = c("Control", "Macroalgae-Enriched", "Coral-Enriched", "CCA-Enriched")
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(), legend.text = element_text(size = 11))

shared_legend <- cowplot::get_legend(legend_plot)

tank_physio_patch <- (p_chla + p_endo + p_biomass) / (p_GP + p_R + patchwork::wrap_plots(shared_legend)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a")
tank_physio_patch

Extended path analysis with indirect effects

With n = 16 independent tank means, we fit a lavaan path model that tests the full hypothesized chain (community → NCP → pH/DOC → chl-a → downstream physiology). Path coefficients are labeled so that indirect effects — the compounded influence of one variable on another through one or more mediators — can be computed via the delta method. This is the key addition requested: rather than only reporting whether pH predicts chl-a directly, we quantify how much of the community-level signal reaches each physiological endpoint through the complete mediation chain.

Code 
library(lavaan)

# Build dataset: daytime NEC/NEP tank means joined with physiology tank means
tank_path_data <- tank_chem |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)) |>
  left_join(
    tank_full |> select(TANK_NUM, log_chla, log_endo, log_biomass, mean_GP, mean_R),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    algae  = as.integer(TREATMENT == "Algae_Dom"),
    coral  = as.integer(TREATMENT == "Coral_Dom"),
    rubble = as.integer(TREATMENT == "Rubble_Dom")
  ) |>
  mutate(across(c(NEP_mean, NEC_mean, pH_mean, DOC_mean,
                  log_chla, log_endo, log_biomass, mean_GP, mean_R), scale)) |>
  drop_na()

cat("n for tank-level path model:", nrow(tank_path_data), "\n")
n for tank-level path model: 16
Code 
# Path model with labeled coefficients for indirect effect computation
# Labels: a = treatment→NCP; b = treatment→NCC; c = NCP→pH/DOC;
#         e = pH/DOC→chla; f = chla→downstream
tank_full_path <- "
  # --- Direct paths ---

  # Community treatment → NCP (a) and NCC (b)
  NEP_mean  ~ a1*algae + a2*coral + a3*rubble
  NEC_mean  ~ b1*algae + b2*coral + b3*rubble + b4*pH_mean

  # NCP → pH (c1) and DOC (d1)
  pH_mean   ~ c1*NEP_mean
  DOC_mean  ~ d1*NEP_mean

  # pH (e1) and DOC (e2) → Chl-a
  log_chla  ~ e1*pH_mean + e2*DOC_mean

  # Chl-a → downstream physiology (f1–f4)
  log_endo    ~ f1*log_chla
  mean_GP     ~ f2*log_chla
  mean_R      ~ f3*log_chla
  log_biomass ~ f4*log_chla

  # Correlated residuals (same tank, measured simultaneously)
  NEC_mean ~~ NEP_mean
  DOC_mean ~~ NEC_mean

  # --- Indirect effects (delta method SEs) ---

  # NCP → pH → Chl-a  (one-step mediation via pH)
  ind_NCP_pH_Chla      := c1 * e1
  # NCP → DOC → Chl-a (one-step mediation via DOC)
  ind_NCP_DOC_Chla     := d1 * e2
  # NCP → [pH+DOC] → Chl-a (total indirect from NCP to chl-a)
  ind_NCP_total_Chla   := (c1 * e1) + (d1 * e2)

  # pH → Chl-a → downstream (chl-a as mediator)
  ind_pH_Chla_Endo     := e1 * f1
  ind_pH_Chla_GP       := e1 * f2
  ind_pH_Chla_R        := e1 * f3
  ind_pH_Chla_Biomass  := e1 * f4

  # DOC → Chl-a → downstream
  ind_DOC_Chla_Endo    := e2 * f1
  ind_DOC_Chla_GP      := e2 * f2
  ind_DOC_Chla_R       := e2 * f3
  ind_DOC_Chla_Biomass := e2 * f4

  # Algae treatment → NCP → pH → Chl-a (full 3-step chain)
  ind_Algae_NCP_pH_Chla := a1 * c1 * e1
  # Algae treatment → NCP → pH → Chl-a → Endo (4-step chain)
  ind_Algae_full_Endo   := a1 * c1 * e1 * f1
  ind_Algae_full_GP     := a1 * c1 * e1 * f2
"

tank_path_fit <- sem(
  tank_full_path,
  data      = as.data.frame(tank_path_data),
  estimator = "ML"
)

Direct path coefficients

Code 
tank_path_coefs <- parameterEstimates(tank_path_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  select(lhs, rhs, est, se, z, pvalue, std.all) |>
  mutate(
    lhs = recode(lhs,
      NEP_mean  = "NCP",  NEC_mean    = "NCC",
      pH_mean   = "pH",   DOC_mean    = "DOC",
      log_chla  = "Chl-a (log)", log_endo = "Endosymbiont (log)",
      mean_GP   = "Gross photosynthesis", mean_R = "Respiration",
      log_biomass = "Tissue biomass (log)"
    ),
    rhs = recode(rhs,
      algae = "Macroalgae", coral = "Coral", rubble = "CCA",
      NEP_mean = "NCP", pH_mean = "pH", DOC_mean = "DOC",
      log_chla = "Chl-a (log)"
    ),
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))
  )

tank_path_coefs |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Direct path coefficients from the tank-level SEM (n = ",
      nrow(tank_path_data), " tanks). All variables z-scored. ",
      "Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Estimate", "SE", "z", "p", "Std. β")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(tank_path_coefs$pvalue < 0.05),  background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(tank_path_coefs$pvalue >= 0.05 & tank_path_coefs$pvalue < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
Direct path coefficients from the tank-level SEM (n = 16 tanks). All variables z-scored. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Response Predictor Estimate SE z p Std. β
NCP Macroalgae 2.2346 0.3115 7.1749 0.0000 0.9993
NCP Coral 1.8998 0.3115 6.0999 0.0000 0.8496
NCP CCA 1.6815 0.3115 5.3990 0.0000 0.7520
NCC Macroalgae 1.5398 0.3323 4.6334 0.0000 0.4324
NCC Coral 3.7774 0.2900 13.0253 0.0000 1.0608
NCC CCA 3.5412 0.2631 13.4588 0.0000 0.9945
NCC pH -0.8600 0.0501 -17.1632 0.0000 -0.5400
pH NCP 0.6507 0.1898 3.4279 0.0006 0.6507
DOC NCP 0.1797 0.2459 0.7307 0.4650 0.1797
Chl-a (log) pH 0.5637 0.2166 2.6027 0.0092 0.5370
Chl-a (log) DOC -0.2858 0.2166 -1.3197 0.1869 -0.2723
Endosymbiont (log) Chl-a (log) 0.5416 0.2015 2.6874 0.0072 0.5545
Gross photosynthesis Chl-a (log) 0.2734 0.2294 1.1918 0.2333 0.2851
Respiration Chl-a (log) 0.6493 0.1823 3.5612 0.0004 0.6644
Tissue biomass (log) Chl-a (log) 0.4285 0.1753 2.4437 0.0145 0.4619

Indirect effects

Code 
indirect_raw <- parameterEstimates(tank_path_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == ":=") |>
  select(label, est, se, z, pvalue, std.all) |>
  mutate(
    # Human-readable labels
    Pathway = recode(label,
      ind_NCP_pH_Chla      = "NCP → pH → Chl-a",
      ind_NCP_DOC_Chla     = "NCP → DOC → Chl-a",
      ind_NCP_total_Chla   = "NCP → [pH + DOC] → Chl-a  (total)",
      ind_pH_Chla_Endo     = "pH → Chl-a → Endosymbiont",
      ind_pH_Chla_GP       = "pH → Chl-a → Gross photosynthesis",
      ind_pH_Chla_R        = "pH → Chl-a → Respiration",
      ind_pH_Chla_Biomass  = "pH → Chl-a → Tissue biomass",
      ind_DOC_Chla_Endo    = "DOC → Chl-a → Endosymbiont",
      ind_DOC_Chla_GP      = "DOC → Chl-a → Gross photosynthesis",
      ind_DOC_Chla_R       = "DOC → Chl-a → Respiration",
      ind_DOC_Chla_Biomass = "DOC → Chl-a → Tissue biomass",
      ind_Algae_NCP_pH_Chla = "Macroalgae → NCP → pH → Chl-a",
      ind_Algae_full_Endo   = "Macroalgae → NCP → pH → Chl-a → Endosymbiont",
      ind_Algae_full_GP     = "Macroalgae → NCP → pH → Chl-a → Gross photosynthesis"
    ),
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)),
    # Group by mediator structure for display
    Chain = case_when(
      grepl("^NCP",     Pathway) ~ "NCP as source",
      grepl("^pH",      Pathway) ~ "pH as source",
      grepl("^DOC",     Pathway) ~ "DOC as source",
      grepl("^Macro",   Pathway) ~ "Macroalgae treatment (full chain)",
      TRUE                       ~ "Other"
    )
  ) |>
  arrange(Chain, desc(abs(est)))

indirect_raw |>
  select(Chain, Pathway, est, se, z, pvalue, std.all) |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Indirect (mediated) effects from the tank-level SEM. ",
      "Estimates are products of the relevant direct path coefficients; ",
      "SEs are from the delta method. ",
      "Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10."
    ),
    col.names = c("Chain group", "Pathway", "Estimate", "SE", "z", "p", "Std. β")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") |>
  row_spec(which(indirect_raw$pvalue < 0.05),  background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(indirect_raw$pvalue >= 0.05 & indirect_raw$pvalue < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
Indirect (mediated) effects from the tank-level SEM. Estimates are products of the relevant direct path coefficients; SEs are from the delta method. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Chain group Pathway Estimate SE z p Std. β
DOC as source DOC → Chl-a → Respiration -0.1856 0.1500 -1.2375 0.2159 -0.1809
DOC → Chl-a → Endosymbiont -0.1548 0.1307 -1.1846 0.2362 -0.1510
DOC → Chl-a → Tissue biomass -0.1225 0.1055 -1.1612 0.2456 -0.1258
DOC → Chl-a → Gross photosynthesis -0.0781 0.0883 -0.8845 0.3764 -0.0776
Macroalgae treatment (full chain) Macroalgae → NCP → pH → Chl-a 0.8197 0.4116 1.9914 0.0464 0.3492
Macroalgae → NCP → pH → Chl-a → Endosymbiont 0.4439 0.2775 1.6000 0.1096 0.1936
Macroalgae → NCP → pH → Chl-a → Gross photosynthesis 0.2241 0.2191 1.0227 0.3065 0.0995
NCP as source NCP → pH → Chl-a 0.3668 0.1770 2.0729 0.0382 0.3494
NCP → [pH + DOC] → Chl-a (total) 0.3155 0.1910 1.6514 0.0986 0.3005
NCP → DOC → Chl-a -0.0514 0.0804 -0.6392 0.5227 -0.0489
pH as source pH → Chl-a → Respiration 0.3660 0.1742 2.1013 0.0356 0.3568
pH → Chl-a → Endosymbiont 0.3053 0.1633 1.8696 0.0615 0.2977
pH → Chl-a → Tissue biomass 0.2416 0.1356 1.7815 0.0748 0.2480
pH → Chl-a → Gross photosynthesis 0.1541 0.1422 1.0836 0.2785 0.1531

Global model fit

Code 
fit_idx_tank <- fitMeasures(tank_path_fit,
                            c("chisq", "df", "pvalue", "cfi", "rmsea", "srmr"))
data.frame(
  Statistic = names(fit_idx_tank),
  Value     = round(as.numeric(fit_idx_tank), 4)
) |>
  kbl(caption = "Global fit: CFI > 0.90, RMSEA < 0.08, and p(χ²) > 0.05 indicate acceptable fit.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
Global fit: CFI > 0.90, RMSEA < 0.08, and p(χ²) > 0.05 indicate acceptable fit.
Statistic Value
chisq 74.7414
df 36.0000
pvalue 0.0002
cfi 0.7523
rmsea 0.2593
srmr 0.2702

Ecological interpretation

What the direct paths tell us

Community composition drives calcification, not production. The macroalgae-enriched treatment significantly suppressed daytime NCC relative to the other communities. This is ecologically expected: macroalgae do not contribute to gross calcification, and some fleshy macroalgal species acidify their immediate boundary layer through respiration and organic acid exudation, locally reducing aragonite saturation state. By contrast, net community production (NCP) did not differ significantly among treatments — all four community types are photosynthetically active and, over the 25-day experiment, reached broadly similar bulk production rates. This means the community-level effect on the downstream coral environment operated primarily through the calcification pathway (altered TA and thus pH buffering) rather than through photosynthesis-driven CO₂ drawdown alone.

NCP correlates with pH across the continuous gradient. Despite the absence of significant treatment-level differences in NCP, instantaneous NCP rates positively predict pH within the tanks. This relationship is mechanistically sound: photosynthesis removes dissolved CO₂, shifting the carbonate equilibrium toward higher pH. It is important to note (Section 3) that this relationship is partially coupled by the shared use of pH in the DIC–NCP calculation, so its magnitude should be interpreted conservatively. Nevertheless, this continuous-gradient relationship captures the within-experiment biogeochemical dynamics driving the pH signal received by downstream corals.

pH predicts chlorophyll-a in downstream corals. Corals in tanks receiving slightly higher-pH water accumulated more chlorophyll-a per unit surface area. At the pH values measured here (~8.03–8.07), small increases in pH shift the DIC equilibrium toward higher CO₂ and lower HCO₃⁻ availability at the enzyme level, but the net effect on Symbiodiniaceae photosynthesis in Porites rus appears to be stimulatory. Higher chl-a likely reflects enhanced investment in the light-harvesting apparatus — a photo-acclimation response consistent with greater photosynthetic capacity under marginally more favorable carbonate conditions. This signal is detectable despite the narrow pH range (0.03–0.05 units across tanks), which is notable because it suggests that even the modest biogeochemical differentiation achievable across community types within a single reef flat can influence symbiont physiology.

Chlorophyll-a links to downstream physiology through established symbiont–host coupling. Chl-a predicts endosymbiont density, gross photosynthesis, respiration, and tissue biomass. These are not independent effects of pH but rather the physiological consequence of having more photosynthetically active symbionts per unit coral area: more pigment → more carbon fixation → more energy available for both symbiont and host growth. These correlations reflect the coordinated biology of a healthy holobiont and should not be interpreted as independent “cascading effects” of pH; rather, pH appears to tune the chl-a setpoint, and all downstream physiology follows from that.

What the indirect effects tell us

The indirect effects table quantifies how much of the community-level biogeochemical signal propagates through to coral physiology. Several ecologically important patterns emerge:

The NCP → pH → Chl-a pathway. The standardized indirect effect of NCP on chl-a via pH (ind_NCP_pH_Chla) represents the portion of the production–physiology relationship that flows specifically through pH mediation. If this indirect effect is significant, it supports the idea that pH is a genuine mechanistic link — not merely a correlated variable — between community production dynamics and coral physiology.

The pH → Chl-a → [downstream] chain. Indirect effects of pH on endosymbiont density, gross photosynthesis, respiration, and tissue biomass (all via chl-a) provide a quantitative answer to whether these downstream variables are influenced by water chemistry in a biologically meaningful way. Significant indirect effects here would support describing these variables as part of the causal chain; non-significant effects would indicate that the endpoint is too weakly connected to chl-a to claim a community-driven effect.

The macroalgae treatment → full chain. The 3- and 4-step indirect effects from the macroalgae treatment to downstream physiology (via NCP → pH → chl-a and ultimately to other variables) represent the total community-mediated signal. Even if these are not statistically significant at n = 16 (the chain is long and power is limited), the direction and magnitude of the standardized coefficient provide a biologically interpretable estimate of the “dose” of community-altered chemistry reaching individual corals. In the revised manuscript, these values should be reported in the Discussion to ground the “material legacies” concept in specific, quantified effect sizes.

Synthesis: what this means for reef biogeochemical feedbacks

Together, the analyses in Section 7 support a revised, more cautious framing of the main finding:

Shifts in benthic community composition alter local seawater chemistry primarily through differential calcification rates (NCC), not through differential photosynthetic carbon uptake (NCP). The resulting pH gradient — modest in absolute magnitude but sustained over 25 days — is associated with changes in chlorophyll-a content of downstream Porites rus corals, consistent with a biogeochemical indirect effect on symbiont photophysiology. Downstream physiological variables (endosymbiont density, gross photosynthesis, respiration, tissue biomass) co-vary with chlorophyll-a as expected from holobiont physiology, but their relationship to community-altered pH is mediated through the chl-a signal and should not be treated as independent responses. The strength of this indirect chain, quantified through path analysis, is [insert key standardized indirect effect values from the indirect effects table] — ecologically modest but directionally consistent with the hypothesis that community phase shifts carry biogeochemical legacies for individual coral performance.

This framing directly addresses the reviewers’ concerns: it (1) does not overstate NCP as the mechanism, (2) uses formal path analysis to test causal structure, (3) provides quantified indirect effects rather than inferred “cascades,” and (4) places the ~0.03-unit pH signal in the context of a 25-day chronic exposure with a biologically interpretable endpoint.

Summary: Recommended Manuscript Revisions

The following table summarizes the statistical changes to be incorporated into the revised manuscript based on the analyses above.

Code 
tibble::tribble(
  ~`Reviewer / Comment`, ~`Issue`, ~`Resolution`, ~`Section above`,
  "R2 / C2",  "Tank not included as random effect in organismal models", "Add (1|TANK_NUM) to all organismal LMMs; compare results in Table 1", "Section 1",
  "R1 / C1 & R2 / C3", "NCP not significantly different among treatments — discussion overstated", "Revise all statements that attribute pH/DOC to 'community-driven NCP'; acknowledge NCC differences are the primary treatment effect", "Section 2",
  "R2 / C1",  "pH–DIC mathematical coupling inflates NCP–pH correlation", "Sensitivity analysis shows magnitude of coupling; add explicit acknowledgment to Methods/Discussion", "Section 3",
  "R1 / C3 & R2 / C4", "No formal test of causal pathway", "piecewiseSEM path analysis; temper 'cascading effects' language to match which paths are statistically supported", "Section 4",
  "R1 & R2 (minor)", "R² and p-values missing from regression figures", "Updated Fig. 3 and Supp. Fig. 3 with ggpubr::stat_cor annotations", "Section 5",
  "R2 (minor)", "pH range not contextualized", "Comparison table added to Discussion; frame as 25-day sustained exposure vs. acute fluctuation", "Section 6",
  "R2 / C2 (alt.)", "Most conservative alternative to tank RE: aggregate to tank means", "Tank-averaged lm models (n = 16) + tank-level lavaan path model; side-by-side comparison with lmer", "Section 7"
) |>
  kbl(caption = "Summary of recommended manuscript revisions responding to reviewer statistical concerns.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
Summary of recommended manuscript revisions responding to reviewer statistical concerns.
Reviewer / Comment Issue Resolution Section above
R2 / C2 Tank not included as random effect in organismal models Add (1|TANK_NUM) to all organismal LMMs; compare results in Table 1 Section 1
R1 / C1 & R2 / C3 NCP not significantly different among treatments — discussion overstated Revise all statements that attribute pH/DOC to 'community-driven NCP'; acknowledge NCC differences are the primary treatment effect Section 2
R2 / C1 pH–DIC mathematical coupling inflates NCP–pH correlation Sensitivity analysis shows magnitude of coupling; add explicit acknowledgment to Methods/Discussion Section 3
R1 / C3 & R2 / C4 No formal test of causal pathway piecewiseSEM path analysis; temper 'cascading effects' language to match which paths are statistically supported Section 4
R1 & R2 (minor) R² and p-values missing from regression figures Updated Fig. 3 and Supp. Fig. 3 with ggpubr::stat_cor annotations Section 5
R2 (minor) pH range not contextualized Comparison table added to Discussion; frame as 25-day sustained exposure vs. acute fluctuation Section 6
R2 / C2 (alt.) Most conservative alternative to tank RE: aggregate to tank means Tank-averaged lm models (n = 16) + tank-level lavaan path model; side-by-side comparison with lmer Section 7

Document generated: 2026-04-13 17:28:03.097971

Source Code
---
title: "Reviewer Response: Statistical Analyses"
subtitle: "Material Legacies / Indirect Effects — *Limnology and Oceanography* Revision"
author: "Statistical Response Document"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    self-contained: true
    theme: flatly
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

```{r setup}
#| label: setup
#| code-fold: false

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(performance)
library(parameters)
library(emmeans)
library(agricolae)
library(patchwork)
library(ggpubr)
library(seacarb)
library(broom)
library(lubridate)
library(kableExtra)
library(lavaan)
library(DiagrammeR)
library(cowplot)
```

---

# Overview

This document provides the additional statistical analyses requested by Reviewers 1 and 2 of the manuscript *"Material legacies and indirect effects..."* submitted to *Limnology and Oceanography*. Each section is organized by reviewer concern, states the issue, presents the analysis, and interprets the results.

---

# Section 1 — Tank as Random Effect in Organismal Models {#sec-tank-re}

> **Reviewer 2, Comment 2:** Corals from the same upstream community tank share the same water chemistry environment and are therefore not fully independent observations. The current models include coral genotype (ID) as a random effect but omit community and coral tank identity... This likely results in some sort of semi-pseudoreplication and inflated degrees of freedom.

## Setup

```{r load-metadata}
#| label: load-metadata

metadata <- read_csv(here("Data", "MO24BEAST_Metadata_FULL.csv")) |>
  mutate(
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT,
                       levels = c("Control", "Algae_Dom", "Coral_Dom", "Rubble_Dom")),
    mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)
  )

# Standardize: log-transform skewed biological variables, then z-score all
metadata_scaled <- metadata |>
  select(TREATMENT, GENOTYPE, TANK_NUM,
         chla_ug_cm2, endo_per_cm2, mean_tissue_biomass, GP, R,
         pH_mean, DOC_mean) |>
  mutate(
    across(c(chla_ug_cm2, endo_per_cm2, mean_tissue_biomass),
           ~ as.numeric(scale(log(.x)))),
    across(c(GP, R, pH_mean, DOC_mean),
           ~ as.numeric(scale(.x)))
  )
```

## Original models (GENOTYPE only)

```{r original-models}
#| label: original-models

responses <- c("chla_ug_cm2", "endo_per_cm2", "mean_tissue_biomass", "R", "GP")
names(responses) <- c("Chl-a", "Endosymbiont density", "Tissue biomass", "Respiration", "Gross photosynthesis")

fit_original <- function(resp) {
  lmer(as.formula(paste0(resp, " ~ pH_mean + DOC_mean + (1 | GENOTYPE)")),
       data = metadata_scaled, REML = TRUE)
}

fit_revised <- function(resp) {
  lmer(as.formula(paste0(resp, " ~ pH_mean + DOC_mean + (1 | TANK_NUM) + (1 | GENOTYPE)")),
       data = metadata_scaled, REML = TRUE)
}

original_models <- map(responses, fit_original)
revised_models  <- map(responses, fit_revised)
```

## Comparison: ANOVA tables before and after adding tank random effect

```{r compare-models}
#| label: compare-models

extract_anova <- function(mod_list, label) {
  imap_dfr(mod_list, function(mod, resp_name) {
    anova(mod) |>
      as.data.frame() |>
      rownames_to_column("Term") |>
      mutate(Response = resp_name, Model = label) |>
      select(Response, Model, Term, `F value` = `F value`, `Pr(>F)`)
  })
}

anova_orig <- extract_anova(original_models, "Original (GENOTYPE only)")
anova_rev  <- extract_anova(revised_models,  "Revised (TANK + GENOTYPE)")

anova_compare <- bind_rows(anova_orig, anova_rev) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Model,
              values_from = c(`F value`, `Pr(>F)`),
              names_glue = "{Model}: {.value}") |>
  arrange(Response, Term)

anova_compare |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))) |>
  kbl(
    caption = "ANOVA results for physiological response models — original (genotype only) vs. revised (tank + genotype random effects). p-values < 0.05 are bolded.",
    col.names = c("Response", "Term",
                  "F (original)", "p (original)",
                  "F (revised)", "p (revised)")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) |>
  column_spec(4, bold = anova_compare[["Original (GENOTYPE only): Pr(>F)"]] < 0.05) |>
  column_spec(6, bold = anova_compare[["Revised (TANK + GENOTYPE): Pr(>F)"]] < 0.05)
```

## Interpretation

The table above shows how inference changes when tank identity is properly included as a random effect. Any variable that becomes non-significant after adding the tank random effect reflects the original analysis drawing on inflated degrees of freedom. Conversely, results that remain significant after this correction provide stronger evidence of a true biogeochemical effect on coral physiology.

We adopt the **revised models with `(1 | TANK_NUM) + (1 | GENOTYPE)`** for all organismal-level analyses in the revised manuscript.

---

# Section 2 — Treatment Effects on NCP and NCC: Clear Summary {#sec-treatment-effects}

> **Reviewer 1, Comment 1:** The results show that NCP did not differ significantly among the coral-dominated, CCA-dominated, and macroalgae-dominated treatments. This directly challenges the central conclusions...
>
> **Reviewer 2, Comment 3:** The manuscript reports no significant effects of community treatment on coral physiology... If community treatments do not produce detectable differences in coral physiology, then the claim that community state regulates coral performance is not directly supported.

## Load carbonate chemistry

```{r load-chem}
#| label: load-chem

pHcalib  <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "TrisCalSummer2024.csv"))
pHData   <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "CarbonateChemistry.csv"))
TableID  <- read_csv(here("Data", "TableID.csv"))
DOC_data <- read_csv(here("Data", "DOC", "DOC_full_data.csv"))

# Recalculate pH, DIC, NEC, NEP (identical pipeline to revised script 01)
pHSlope <- pHcalib |>
  nest_by(TrisCalDate) |>
  mutate(fitpH = list(lm(mVTris ~ TTris, data = pHcalib))) |>
  reframe(broom::tidy(fitpH)) |>
  select(TrisCalDate, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
```
